title: Spinal Cord Cross-Section (Horns and Columns)
topic: internal organization of the spinal cord
description: OpenStax diagram detailing the specific anatomical subdivisions of the spinal cord's interior. It highlights the functional horns of the gray matter (sensory, autonomic, motor) and the white matter columns (funiculi) that house ascending and descending tracts.
clinical_notes: Specific pathologies target distinct areas of this cross-section. ALS specifically degenerates the anterior horn cells (motor neurons) and lateral columns (corticospinal tracts). OT intervention for ALS is heavily compensatory, focusing on energy conservation, environmental modifications, and progressive assistive technology as motor function declines.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 14 – The Brain and Cranial Nerves / Chapter 13
